generator.disclaimer: The draft is not an official translation and is not itself legal advice. Corpus annotators add the project translation metadata and pin the source/version boundary.

draftParagraphs:
(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date of promulgation. However, the provisions of Chapters 4 to 6 and Articles 2 to 6 of the Supplementary Provisions shall come into effect from the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding two years from the date of promulgation.

(Transitional measures regarding consent of the person)

Article 2 In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given before the enforcement of this Act, if that consent corresponds to consent to allow the handling of personal information for purposes other than the purpose of use specified pursuant to the provisions of Article 15, paragraph (1), it shall be deemed that the consent provided for in Article 16, paragraph (1) or paragraph (2) has been given.

Article 3 If there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given before the enforcement of this Act, and if that consent is equivalent to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 23, paragraph 1, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in the same paragraph has been given.

(Transitional measures regarding notification)

Article 4 If the person has been notified prior to the enforcement of this Act with respect to matters that must be notified to the person or made readily available to the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 23, paragraph 2, such notification shall be deemed to have been given pursuant to the provisions of the same paragraph.

Article 5 If the person has been notified prior to the enforcement of this Act with regard to matters that must be notified to the person or made readily available to the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 23, Paragraph 5, Item 3, such notification shall be deemed to have been given pursuant to the provisions of the same item.

(Transitional measures regarding restrictions on the use of names)

Article 6 The provisions of Article 45 shall not apply to any person who is currently using the name Certified Personal Information Protection Organization or a name confusingly similar thereto at the time of enforcement of this Act, for six months after the enforcement of the provisions of the same Article.

(Transitional measures regarding anonymously processed information by administrative agencies, etc.)

Article 7 For the time being, the application of the provisions of Article 110 and Article 111 to local government agencies and local independent administrative agencies other than designated cities under Article 252-19, Paragraph 1 of the Prefectural and Local Autonomy Act shall apply. In Article 110, the phrase ``the head of an administrative organ, etc.,'' means ``the head of an administrative organ, etc. intends to recruit pursuant to the provisions of the following article,'' and the phrase ``shall'' in Article 111 means ``may.''

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date of enforcement of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information Held by Administrative Organs.

(Delegation of other transitional measures to Cabinet Order)

Article 4 In addition to what is provided for in the preceding two articles, necessary transitional measures for the enforcement of this Act shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date of enforcement of the Local Independent Administrative Agency Act (Act No. 118 of 2003). However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

(1) The later of the enforcement date of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as stipulated in Article 6 or the enforcement date of this Act.

(Delegation of other transitional measures to Cabinet Order)

Article 6 In addition to what is provided for in these Supplementary Provisions, transitional measures necessary for the enforcement of this Act shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date of enforcement of the Consumer Affairs Agency and Consumer Commission Establishment Act (Act No. 48 of 2009). However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

Provisions of Article 9 of the Supplementary Provisions Date of promulgation of this Act

(Transitional measures regarding disposition, etc.)

Article 4 Licenses, permissions, approvals, approvals, designations and other dispositions, notifications and other acts performed prior to the enforcement of this Act pursuant to the provisions of the respective laws (including orders based thereon; hereinafter referred to as "old laws and regulations") prior to amendment by this Law are not otherwise provided for in laws and regulations. In addition to the above, after the enforcement of this Act, such acts shall be deemed to be licenses, permissions, authorizations, approvals, designations and other dispositions, notifications, and other acts made pursuant to the corresponding provisions of the respective laws revised by this Act (including orders based thereon; hereinafter referred to as "New Laws").

2 Applications for licenses, notifications, and other acts that are currently being carried out pursuant to the provisions of the old laws and regulations at the time of enforcement of this Act shall be deemed to be applications for licenses, notifications, and other acts that have been carried out pursuant to the corresponding provisions of the new laws and regulations after the enforcement of this Act, unless otherwise provided in the laws and regulations.

3. With respect to matters for which reporting, notification, submission, and other procedures must be carried out pursuant to the provisions of the old laws and regulations before the enforcement of this Act, but the procedures have not been completed before the enforcement date of this Act, unless otherwise provided in the laws and regulations, after the enforcement of this Act, the provisions of the new laws and regulations shall be deemed to have not been completed pursuant to the corresponding provisions of the new laws and regulations, and the provisions of the new laws and regulations shall apply.

(Transitional measures regarding the effectiveness of orders)

Article 5 Unless otherwise provided in laws and regulations, the Cabinet Office Ordinance set forth in Article 7, Paragraph 3 of the Cabinet Office Establishment Act or the Ministerial Order set forth in Article 12, Paragraph 1 of the National Administrative Organization Act issued pursuant to the provisions of the old laws and regulations shall have effect as the corresponding Cabinet Office Ordinance set forth in Article 7, Paragraph 3 of the Cabinet Office Establishment Act or Ministerial Order set forth in Article 12, Paragraph 1 of the National Administrative Organization Act issued based on the corresponding provisions of the new Act, unless otherwise provided in the laws and regulations.

(Transitional measures regarding application of penalties)

Article 8. With regard to the application of penal provisions to acts committed before the enforcement of this Act and acts committed after the enforcement of this Act in cases where the supplementary provisions of this Act provide that the provisions then in force shall remain applicable, the provisions then in force shall remain applicable.

(Delegation to Cabinet Order)

Article 9 In addition to what is provided for in Article 2 to the preceding article of the Supplementary Provisions, transitional measures necessary for the enforcement of this Act (including transitional measures regarding penalties) shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as of the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding two years from the date of promulgation. However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

Date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 7, Paragraph 2, Article 10 and Article 12 of the Supplementary Provisions

2 Articles 1 and 4 and Supplementary Provisions Articles 5, 6, Article 7, paragraphs 1 and 3, Article 8, Article 9, Article 13, Article 22, Articles 25 to 27, Articles 30, 32, 34, and 37 January 1, 2016

Sanryaku

The date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year and six months from the date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 4.

(5) Articles 3 and 6 (excluding the revised provisions of Article 19, Item 1 of the Act on the Use of Numbers and Annexed Table 1) and the provisions of Article 19-3, Article 24, Article 29-3 and Article 36 of the Supplementary Provisions. Enforcement date of the provisions listed in Article 1, Item 5 of the Supplementary Provisions of the Act on Number Use.

(Transitional measures regarding notifications, etc.)

Article 2 A person who intends to provide personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 23, Paragraph 2 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as amended pursuant to the provisions of Article 2 (hereinafter referred to as the "New Personal Information Protection Act") shall do so on the date of enforcement of this Act (hereinafter referred to as the "Effective Date"). Even in the above cases, pursuant to the rules of the Personal Information Protection Commission, the person concerned may be notified of matters corresponding to the matters listed in item 5 of the same paragraph, and the personal information protection commission may be notified of matters corresponding to the matters listed in the items of the same paragraph. In this case, such notifications and notifications shall be deemed to be notifications and notifications pursuant to the provisions of the same paragraph after the effective date.

(Transitional measures regarding the consent of the person regarding provision to a third party in a foreign country)

Article 3 If there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given before the enforcement date, and if that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party in a foreign country pursuant to the provisions of Article 24 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, it shall be deemed that consent under the same article has been given.

(Transitional measures regarding dispositions, etc. taken by the competent minister)

Article 4 Prior to the enforcement date, pursuant to the provisions of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information before the amendment pursuant to the provisions of Article 2 (hereinafter referred to as the "Old Personal Information Protection Act") or an order based thereon, the competent minister prescribed in Article 36 or Article 49 of the Old Personal Information Protection Act (hereinafter referred to simply as "Old Personal Information Protection Act") After the effective date, recommendations, orders and other dispositions, notifications and other acts made by the "Competent Minister" shall be deemed to be recommendations, orders and other dispositions, notices and other acts made by the Personal Information Protection Commission based on the relevant provisions of the New Personal Information Protection Act or orders based thereon.

(2) Applications, notifications, and other acts that have been made to the competent minister pursuant to the provisions of the old Personal Information Protection Act or orders based thereon at the time of enforcement of this Act shall, after the date of enforcement, be deemed to be applications, notifications, and other acts made to the Personal Information Protection Commission based on the corresponding provisions of the new Personal Information Protection Act or orders based thereon.

3. For matters that require notification or other procedures to be carried out with the competent minister pursuant to the provisions of the Old Personal Information Protection Act or an order based thereon before the enforcement date, but those procedures have not been completed before the enforcement date, after the enforcement date, such matters shall be deemed to be treated as if the procedures had not been completed with the Personal Information Protection Commission pursuant to the corresponding provisions of the new Personal Information Protection Act or an order based thereon, and the corresponding provisions shall apply.

(Transitional measures regarding appointment, etc. of committee chairperson or committee members)

Article 7 A person who is the chairman or a member of the previous Specific Personal Information Protection Commission at the time of the enforcement of the provisions listed in Article 1, item 2 of the Supplementary Provisions shall be deemed to have been appointed as the chairman or member of the Personal Information Protection Commission on the enforcement date of item 2, respectively, pursuant to the provisions of Article 54, paragraph 3 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as amended pursuant to the provisions of Article 1 (hereinafter referred to in this article as the "New Personal Information Protection Act"). In this case, notwithstanding the provisions of Article 55, Paragraph 1 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, the term of office of the person deemed to have been appointed shall be the same as the remaining term of office as the chairperson or member of the previous Specific Personal Information Protection Commission as of the enforcement date of Item 2.

With regard to the members of the Personal Information Protection Commission who will be newly appointed with the enforcement of the provisions listed in Article 1, Item 2 of the Supplementary Provisions, the acts necessary for the appointment of the members prescribed in Article 54, Paragraph 3 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, Item 2 may be performed even before the enforcement date of Item 2.

3. At the time of the enforcement of the provisions listed in Article 1, item 2 of the Supplementary Provisions, persons who are currently employees of the secretariat of the Specific Personal Information Protection Commission shall become equivalent employees of the secretariat of the Personal Information Protection Commission with the same working conditions on the effective date of item 2, unless a separate appointment is issued.

(Transitional measures regarding application of penalties)

Article 9 With regard to the application of penal provisions to acts committed before the enforcement of this Act (or, in the case of the provisions listed in Article 1, Item 2 of the Supplementary Provisions, said provisions) and acts committed after the enforcement date of Item 2 in cases where the provisions of the preceding Article continue to apply, the provisions then in force shall remain applicable.

(Delegation to Cabinet Order)

Article 10 In addition to what is provided for in these Supplementary Provisions, necessary transitional measures for the enforcement of this Act shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Considerations in formulating guidelines for appropriate and effective implementation of measures to be taken by business operators, etc.)

Article 11 In formulating guidelines for the appropriate and effective implementation of the measures that businesses, etc. should take as stipulated in Article 8 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, the Personal Information Protection Commission shall give particular consideration to ensuring that the business activities of small-scale businesses are carried out smoothly, in view of the fact that with the enforcement of this Act, the persons listed in Article 2, Paragraph 3, Item 5 of the Old Personal Information Protection Act will become business operators handling personal information.

(Consider)

Article 12 By the enforcement date, the government shall, based on the purpose of the provisions of the new Personal Information Protection Act, protect the personal information held by administrative organs stipulated in Article 2, Paragraph 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information Held by Administrative Organs as stipulated in Paragraph 2 of the same Article, and the personal information held by Independent Administrative Agencies, etc. Regulations regarding the handling of personal information as provided in Article 2, Paragraph 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information (Act No. 59 of 2003) held by Independent Administrative Agencies, etc. as provided in Paragraph 2 of the same Article (hereinafter collectively referred to as "Personal Information Retained by Administrative Agencies, etc." in this Article) Anonymously processed information (refers to anonymously processed information as stipulated in Article 2, Paragraph 9 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, and includes anonymously processed information by administrative agencies, etc. (refers to anonymously processed information obtained by processing personal information held by administrative agencies, etc.; hereinafter the same shall apply in this section)) From the perspective of promoting the smooth and prompt use of anonymously processed information, consideration will be given to having the Personal Information Protection Commission provide unified and cross-sectional guidance and advice on the handling of anonymously processed information by administrative agencies, etc., and necessary measures will be taken based on the results.

2. Approximately three years after the enforcement of this Act, the government shall consider improvements to the formulation and promotion of basic policies regarding the protection of personal information and other affairs under the jurisdiction of the Personal Information Protection Commission, taking into account the status of the development of human resources, securing of financial resources, and other measures necessary to carry out these effectively, and, if it deems it necessary, to take necessary measures based on the results.

In addition to the matters stipulated in the preceding paragraph, the government shall, approximately three years after the enforcement of this Act, consider the status of enforcement of the New Personal Information Protection Act, taking into consideration international trends regarding the protection of personal information, progress in information and communication technology, and the creation and development of new industries that utilize personal information, etc., and, if it deems it necessary, take necessary measures based on the results.

4. Approximately three years after the enforcement of the provisions listed in Article 1, Item 6 of the Supplementary Provisions, the government shall ensure that financial institutions prescribed in Article 2, Paragraph 1 of the Deposit Insurance Act (Act No. 34 of 1970) receive money from depositors, etc. prescribed in Article 3 of the same Article, or as prescribed in Article 2, Paragraph 1 of the Agriculture and Fisheries Cooperative Savings Insurance Act (Act No. 53 of 1970). Agricultural and fishery cooperatives shall consider measures to appropriately receive individual numbers from savers, etc. as provided for in paragraph 3 of the same Article, and the status of enforcement of the revised Number Usage Act pursuant to the provisions of Article 7, and if deemed necessary, take necessary measures based on the results and while gaining the understanding of the public.

5. The government shall aim at measures related to cybersecurity (meaning cybersecurity as defined in Article 2 of the Cybersecurity Basic Act (Act No. 14 of 2014)) in order to ensure the safety of personal information held by national administrative organs, etc. In view of the importance of accurate formulation and implementation, consideration will be given to the development of systems for the formulation and implementation of measures based on the standards stipulated in Article 13 of the same law in national administrative organs, etc., and necessary measures will be taken based on the results.

6. The government shall consider the form of legislation regarding the protection of personal information, including the consolidation and integral provision of the provisions regarding the protection of personal information and personal information held by administrative agencies, etc. as stipulated in Article 2, Paragraph 1 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, based on the status of enforcement of the New Personal Information Protection Act, the status of implementation of the measures set forth in Paragraph 1, and other circumstances.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year and six months from the date of promulgation.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as from the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one month from the date of promulgation.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as of the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding three years from the date of promulgation. However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

1 and 2 omitted

(iii) Chapter 11, Article 235, Article 239, paragraph 1 (limited to the part pertaining to item 44), Article 243, paragraph 1 (limited to the part pertaining to item 4 (limited to the part pertaining to Article 239, paragraph 1, item 44)) and paragraph 3, and Article 251 and the Supplementary Provisions. The date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year and six months from the date of promulgation of the provisions of Articles 5, 7 to 10, 12, 14 (limited to the amendment provisions of Article 19, Paragraph 2 of the Act on Promotion of Development of Specified Complex Tourist Facility Areas), 15 and 16.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as of the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding nine months from the date of promulgation.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect as of the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding two years from the date of promulgation. However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

Date of promulgation of the provisions of Articles 9 to 11 of the Supplementary Provisions

(ii) Amended provisions to delete Article 84 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information in Article 1, make Article 83 of the Act into Article 84, and add one article after Article 82 of the Act; amended provisions of Article 85 of the Act; amended provisions of Article 86 of the Act; and amended provisions of Article 87 of the Act; use of numbers to identify specific individuals in administrative procedures in Article 2; The date on which six months have elapsed from the date of promulgation of Article 57 of the relevant Act, Article 46 of the Act on Anonymously Processed Medical Information Contributing to Research and Development in the Medical Field in Article 3, the amendment of adding one article after Article 46 of the same Act, the amended provisions of Article 48 of the same Act, the amended provisions of Article 49 of the same Act, and the provisions of Article 8 of the Supplementary Provisions.

The date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year and six months from the date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 3 and Article 7 of the Supplementary Provisions.

(Transitional measures regarding notifications, etc.)

Article 2 A person who intends to provide personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 23, Paragraph 2 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the amendment pursuant to the provisions of Article 1 (hereinafter referred to as the "New Personal Information Protection Act") shall be entitled to ), pursuant to the rules of the Personal Information Protection Commission, the person may be notified and the Personal Information Protection Commission may be notified of matters corresponding to items 1, 4, and 8 of the same paragraph. In this case, such notifications and notifications shall be deemed to be notifications and notifications pursuant to the provisions of the same paragraph after the effective date.

Article 3 If the person is notified of matters corresponding to the address of the person responsible for the management of personal data as prescribed in Article 23, Paragraph 5, Item 3 of the New Personal Information Protection Act and, in the case of a corporation, the name of its representative, before the enforcement date, such notification shall be deemed to have been made pursuant to the provisions of the same item.

(Transitional measures regarding the provision of information to third parties in foreign countries)

Article 4 The provisions of Article 24, Paragraph 2 of the New Personal Information Protection Act shall apply in cases where a personal information handling business operator obtains the consent of the person pursuant to the provisions of Paragraph 1 of the same article after the effective date.

2. The provisions of Article 24, Paragraph 3 of the New Personal Information Protection Act shall apply to cases where a personal information handling business entity provides personal data to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the date of enforcement.

(Transitional measures regarding the consent of the person concerned with the provision of personal information to third parties)

Article 5 If there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given before the enforcement date, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal information to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 26-2, Paragraph 1 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in Item 1 of the same paragraph has been given.

2. The provisions of Article 24, Paragraph 3 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, as applied mutatis mutandis by replacing the terms in Article 26-2, Paragraph 2 of the New Personal Information Protection Act, shall apply in cases where a personal information handling business operator provides personal information to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the enforcement date.

(Transitional measures regarding businesses covered by certified personal information protection organizations)

Article 6 With regard to personal information handling business operators, etc. that are members of certified personal information protection organizations at the time of enforcement of this Act, the provisions of Article 51, Paragraph 1 of the New Personal Information Protection Act shall be deemed to have been given as of the date of enforcement, and the provisions of the same paragraph shall apply.

(Transitional measures regarding application of penalties)

Article 8 With regard to the application of penalties to acts committed before the enforcement of this Act (or, in the case of the provisions listed in Article 1, Item 2 of the Supplementary Provisions, such provisions), the provisions then in force shall remain applicable.

(Delegation to Cabinet Order)

Article 9 In addition to what is provided for in these Supplementary Provisions, necessary transitional measures for the enforcement of this Act shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Consider)

Article 10: Approximately three years after the enforcement of this Act, the government shall consider the status of enforcement of the New Personal Information Protection Act, taking into consideration international trends regarding the protection of personal information, progress in information and communication technology, and the status of the creation and development of new industries that utilize personal information, etc., and, if it deems it necessary, shall take necessary measures based on the results.

(Effective date)

Article 1 This Act shall come into effect from September 1, 2021. However, the provisions listed in each of the following items shall come into effect from the date specified in each of the items.

1 Article 27 (limited to the revised provisions of Appended Tables 1 to 5 of the Basic Resident Register Act), Article 45, Article 47, and Article 55 (Appended Table of the Act on the Use of Numbers to Identify Specific Individuals in Administrative Procedures) The date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 8, Paragraph 1, Articles 59 to 63, Article 67, and Articles 71 to 73 of the Supplementary Provisions.

two omitted

The date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding nine months from the date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 7, Paragraph 3 of the Three Supplementary Provisions.

(iv) Articles 17, 35, 44, 50, and 58, the following article, Supplementary Provisions Article 3, Article 5, Article 6, Article 7 (excluding paragraph 3), Article 13, Article 14, and Article 18 (limited to the amended provisions of Article 129 of the Family Register Act (excluding the part that adds ``original and'' under ``family register'')), 10. Article 9 to Article 21, Article 23, Article 24, Article 27, Article 29 (excluding the amendment provisions of Article 30-15, Paragraph 3 of the Basic Resident Register Act), Article 30, Article 31, Article 33 to Article 35, Article 40, Article 42, Article 44 to Article 46, Article 48, 50. Articles 52 to 52, Article 53 (excluding the revised provisions of Article 45-2 Paragraphs 1, 5, 6 and 9 of the Act on the Use of Numbers to Identify Specific Individuals in Administrative Procedures, and the revised provisions of Article 52-3 of the same Act), Article 55 (Act on Promotion of Cancer Registration, etc. (Heisei 2) (Act No. 111 of 2015) The revised provisions of Article 35 (excluding the part where "(including ordinances)" is omitted), Article 56, Article 58, Article 64, Article 65, Article 68, and Article 69, the date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year from the date of promulgation of the provisions.

Five strategies

The date specified by Cabinet Order within a period not exceeding one year and six months from the date of promulgation of the provisions of Article 8, Paragraph 2 and Article 9, Paragraph 3 of the Six Supplementary Provisions.

Article 7, Article 27 (limited to the revised provisions of Article 24-2 of the Basic Resident Registration Act and the revised provisions of Article 30-15, Paragraph 3 of the same Act), Article 48 (Article 71-2 of the Act on Authentication Operations of the Local Government Information System Organization regarding Electronic Signatures, etc.) Article 49 and Article 51 of the Act, Article 9 of the Supplementary Provisions (excluding Paragraph 3), Article 10, Article 15, and Article 18 (Revised provisions of Article 129 of the Family Register Act (the part that adds ``original and'' under ``family register'') ), Article 22, Article 25, Article 26, Article 28, Article 29 (limited to the revised provisions of Article 30-15, Paragraph 3 of the Basic Resident Register Act), Article 39, Article 43, Article 47, Article 49, Article 54, Article 55 (Cancer). The revised provisions of Article 35 of the Act on Promotion of Registration, etc. (limited to the part where "(including ordinances)" is omitted), Article 57, Article 66, and Article 70. Within a period not exceeding two years from the date of promulgation, the date specified by Cabinet Order for each provision.

(Transitional measures accompanying enforcement of the provisions of Article 50)

Article 7 Before the enforcement date of the provisions of Article 50 (hereinafter referred to as the "Article 50 Enforcement Date" in this Article), a corporation listed in Appended Table 2, etc. A business operator handling personal information as stipulated in Article 16, Paragraph 2 of the revised Personal Information Protection Act, a business operator handling pseudonymized information as stipulated in Paragraph 5 of the same article, or an independent administrative agency deemed to be a business handling personal information as stipulated in Paragraph 7 of the same Article, or as stipulated in Paragraph 8 of the same Article. (hereinafter the same shall apply in this Article) refers to a business entity handling personal information prescribed in Paragraph 2 of the same Article, which is an academic research institution, etc., as provided for in Article 17 of the Personal Information Protection Act after the Article 50 Amendment. If the consent corresponds to permission to handle personal information for a purpose other than the purpose of use specified pursuant to the provisions of paragraph 1, it shall be deemed that consent under Article 18, paragraph 1 or 2 of the revised Personal Information Protection Act has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 50.

2. In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given to a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 prior to the enforcement date of Article 50, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, Paragraph 1 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information of Article 50, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in the same paragraph has been given as of the Enforcement Date of Article 50.

3 Article 50 Appended Table 2 corporations, etc. that intend to provide personal data to third parties pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information may, even before the enforcement date of Article 50, notify the person and notify the Personal Information Protection Commission of matters corresponding to the matters listed in the items of the same paragraph, pursuant to the rules of the Personal Information Protection Commission. In this case, such notifications and notifications shall be deemed to be notifications and notifications pursuant to the provisions of the same paragraph after the effective date of Article 50.

Article 50 If the person is notified by a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 before the enforcement date of Article 50 with respect to matters that must be notified to the person or made easily accessible to the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, paragraph 5, item 3 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information, such notification shall, after the enforcement date of Article 50, be deemed to be a notice pursuant to the provisions of the same item.

5 In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person given to a corporation in Appended Table 2 prior to the enforcement date of Article 50, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party in a foreign country pursuant to the provisions of Article 28, paragraph (1) of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information of Article 50, the consent provided for in the same paragraph shall be deemed to have been given as of the enforcement date of Article 50.

6 Article 50 The provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information shall apply in the case where a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 obtains the consent of the principal pursuant to the provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment of Article 50 after the enforcement date of Article 50.

7 Article 50 The provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 3 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act shall apply in cases where a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 provides personal data to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the enforcement date of Article 50.

8. In the event that consent regarding the handling of personal information of the principal has been given to a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 prior to the enforcement date of Article 50, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal information to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 31, Paragraph 1, Item 1 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information of Article 50, the consent provided for in the same item shall be deemed to have been given as of the enforcement date of Article 50.

9. The provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 3 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information in Article 50, as applied mutatis mutandis in Article 31, Paragraph 2 of the Amended Act on the Protection of Personal Information in Article 50, shall apply in cases where a corporation, etc. in Appended Table 2 provides personal information to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph on or after the effective date of Article 50.

10 Personal information of the person provided to administrative agencies, etc., as prescribed in Article 2, Paragraph 11 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information, Article 50 (excluding the Japan Labor Health and Safety Agency, which is deemed to be a personal information handling business operator as defined in Paragraph 2, Article 16 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information, as amended in Article 50, pursuant to the provisions of Paragraph 2, Article 58 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information, as amended in Article 50, hereinafter referred to as "Administrative Organs, etc." in this Article) before the enforcement date of Article 50. In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of information, if that consent corresponds to consent to allow the person to use or provide the retained personal information for purposes other than the purpose of use specified pursuant to the provisions of Article 50 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information, Article 61, paragraph 1, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in Article 69, Paragraph 2, Item 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as amended by Article 50 has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 50.

11 In cases where consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person has been given to an administrative agency, etc. prior to the enforcement date of Article 50, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of retained personal information to a third party in a foreign country pursuant to the provisions of Article 71, paragraph (1) of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information, it shall be deemed that the consent provided for in the same paragraph has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 50.

12 Article 50 The provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information apply to cases where an administrative organ, etc. obtains the consent of the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment to Article 50 on or after the effective date of Article 50.

13 Article 50 The provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 3 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act shall apply in cases where an administrative organ, etc. provides retained personal information to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the enforcement date of Article 50.

14 Article 50, Article 74, Paragraph 1 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information regarding the personal information files prescribed in Article 60, Paragraph 2 of the Amended Act on the Protection of Personal Information in Article 50, which are currently held by the administrative organs stipulated in Paragraph 8, Article 2 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information, as amended in Article 50 as of the enforcement date of Article 50. Regarding the application of the provisions, the term "to possess" in the same paragraph shall mean "has been possessed," and the term "in advance" shall mean "without delay after the enforcement of the provisions of Article 50 of the Act on the Development of Related Laws for Forming a Digital Society (Act No. 37 of 2021)."

(Preparatory acts accompanying enforcement of the provisions of Article 51)

Article 8 In order to ensure the proper handling of personal information held by local government agencies and local independent administrative agencies pursuant to the provisions of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as amended pursuant to the provisions of Article 51 (hereinafter referred to as the ``Article 51 Amended Personal Information Protection Act'' in this article, the following article, and Article 10, Paragraph 1 of the Supplementary Provisions), the State shall After understanding the implementation status of preparatory acts necessary for the enforcement of the Article 51 revised Personal Information Protection Act in local government agencies and local independent administrative agencies, by requesting the submission of necessary materials from organizations and other means, and when it is deemed necessary, the Minister shall provide technical advice or recommendations regarding such preparatory acts.

2 Article 51 A notification pursuant to the provisions of Article 167, paragraph (1) of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act may be made even before the enforcement date of the provisions of Article 51 (referred to as the "Article 51 Enforcement Date" in the following article).

(Transitional measures accompanying enforcement of the provisions of Article 51)

Article 9 Before the enforcement date of Article 51, Specified Local Independent Administrative Agencies, etc. (persons listed in Article 58, Paragraph 1, Item 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information in Article 51, or those listed in Paragraph 2 of Article 16 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information as amended in Article 51 pursuant to the provisions of Paragraph 2 of the same Article) A business entity handling personal information as prescribed in Article 51, a business entity handling pseudonymized information as prescribed in paragraph 5 of the same article, or a business entity handling personal information as prescribed in paragraph 7 of the same article. Listed in Article 58, paragraph 2, item 1 of the revised Personal Information Protection Act. (hereinafter the same shall apply in this Article), if such consent corresponds to consent to the handling of personal information for purposes other than the purpose of use specified pursuant to the provisions of Article 51 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act, the consent provided for in Article 18, Paragraph 1 or 2 of the Article 51 Revised Personal Information Protection Act shall be deemed to have been given as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

2. In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of a person given to a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. prior to the enforcement date of Article 51, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, Paragraph 1 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act, Article 51 shall be deemed to have given the consent provided for in the same paragraph as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

3 Article 51 A Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. that intends to provide personal data to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information may, even before the enforcement date of Article 51, notify the person and notify the Personal Information Protection Commission of matters corresponding to the matters listed in the items of the same paragraph, pursuant to the rules of the Personal Information Protection Commission. In this case, such notifications and notifications shall be deemed to be notifications and notifications pursuant to the provisions of the same paragraph after the enforcement date of Article 51.

4 Article 51 If a specified local independent administrative agency, etc. has notified the person of matters that must be notified to the person or made readily available to the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, paragraph 5, item 3 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information pursuant to the provisions of Article 27, Paragraph 5, Item 3 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information, before the enforcement date of Article 51, such notification shall be deemed to be a notice pursuant to the provisions of the same item after the enforcement date of Article 51.

5. In cases where consent regarding the handling of personal information of a person has been given to a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. prior to the enforcement date of Article 51, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal data to a third party in a foreign country pursuant to the provisions of Article 28, paragraph (1) of the Personal Information Protection Act after the amendment to Article 51, it shall be deemed that the consent provided for in the same paragraph has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

6 Article 51 The provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information shall apply to cases where a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. obtains the consent of the person pursuant to the provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 1 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment of Article 51 on or after the effective date of Article 51.

7 Article 51 The provisions of Article 28, Paragraph 3 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act shall apply in cases where a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. provides personal data to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the enforcement date of Article 51.

8. In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of a person given to a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. prior to the enforcement date of Article 51, and that consent corresponds to consent to permit the provision of personal information to a third party pursuant to the provisions of Article 31, paragraph (1), item (i) of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act of Article 51, it shall be deemed that the consent under the same item has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

9 Article 51 The provisions of Article 28, paragraph (3) of the Revised Personal Information Protection Act, as applied mutatis mutandis in Article 31, paragraph (2) of the Article 51 Amended Personal Information Protection Act, shall apply in cases where a Specified Local Independent Administrative Agency, etc. provides personal information to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in the same paragraph after the enforcement date of Article 51.

10 Before the enforcement date of Article 51, the persons listed in Article 2, Paragraph 11, Item 2 or 4 of the Personal Information Protection Act after Article 51 (as per the provisions of Article 58, Paragraph 2 of the Personal Information Protection Act after Article 51) Excluding the persons listed in Article 58, Paragraph 2, Item 1 of the Revised Personal Information Protection Law (Article 51) who are deemed to be personal information handling business operators prescribed in Article 16, Paragraph 2 of the Personal Information Protection Law (hereinafter the same shall apply in this Article). In cases where there is consent regarding the handling of personal information of the person concerned, if that consent corresponds to consent to allow the person to use or provide the retained personal information for purposes other than the purpose of use specified pursuant to the provisions of Article 51 Revised Personal Information Protection Act, Article 51, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in Article 69, Paragraph 2, Item 1 of the Amendment Personal Information Protection Act has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

11 If consent regarding the handling of the person's personal information has been given to a person listed in Article 2, Paragraph 11, Item 2 or 4 of the Personal Information Protection Act after Article 51 as amended before the enforcement date of Article 51, such consent shall be subject to Article 51 as amended. If it corresponds to consent to permit the provision of retained personal information to a third party located in a foreign country pursuant to the provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 1 of the Personal Information Protection Act, it shall be deemed that the consent set forth in the same paragraph has been given as of the enforcement date of Article 51.

12 Article 51 The provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 2 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information shall apply in the case where the person listed in Article 2, Paragraph 11, Item 2 or Item 4 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment to Article 51 obtains the consent of the person pursuant to the provisions of Paragraph 1 of Article 71 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment of Article 51 after the enforcement date of Article 51.

13 Article 51 The provisions of Article 71, Paragraph 3 of the Revised Act on the Protection of Personal Information shall apply in cases where the person listed in Article 2, Paragraph 11, Item 2 or Item 4 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment to Article 51 provides retained personal information to a third party located in a foreign country as provided in Paragraph 3 of Article 71 of the Act on the Protection of Personal Information after the Amendment to Article 51 after the enforcement date of Article 51.

(Relationship between Article 51 and the Ordinance)

Article 10 The provisions of local government ordinances that provide for the punishment of acts regulated by the revised Article 51 Act on the Protection of Personal Information shall cease to be effective as soon as the provisions of Article 51 come into effect.

2 In the event that the provisions of an ordinance lose their effect pursuant to the provisions of the preceding paragraph, and unless the local government provides otherwise in the ordinance, the provisions then in force shall continue to apply even after the expiry with respect to the punishment of violations committed before the expiry of the ordinance.

(Transitional measures regarding penalties)

Article 71 With regard to the application of penal provisions to acts committed before the enforcement of this Act (for the provisions listed in each item of Article 1 of the Supplementary Provisions; the same shall apply hereinafter in this Article) and acts committed after the enforcement of this Act in cases where the provisions then in force remain applicable pursuant to the provisions of this Supplementary Provisions, the provisions then in force shall remain applicable.

(Delegation to Cabinet Order)

Article 72 In addition to what is provided for in these Supplementary Provisions, necessary transitional measures for the enforcement of this Act (including transitional measures regarding penalties) shall be specified by Cabinet Order.

(Consider)

Article 73 The government shall use hiragana or katakana to identify individuals in applications, notifications, notices of disposition, and other procedures related to administrative organs, etc. Specific measures will be considered within one year after the promulgation of this law, including the inclusion of items written in kana or katakana as entries in family registers, and necessary measures will be taken based on the results.
